title: Chart Types
text: myIO supports 17 chart types via type argument addIoLayer(). vignette shows example . examples use built-R datasets can copy run directly.

title: Chart Types
text: Chart types organized compatibility groups. can freely combine types within compatible groups (e.g. point + line + area), standalone types like treemap gauge mixed types. Continuous, categorical, binned groups can combined . Matrix standalone groups mixed groups.

title: Chart Types
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     color = "steelblue",     label = "temperature",     data = airquality,     mapping = list(x_var = "Day", y_var = "Temp")   )

title: Chart Types
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "coral",     label = "scatter",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   )

title: Chart Types
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "bar",     color = "steelblue",     label = "bars",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE)

title: Chart Types
text: Area charts require x_var, low_y, high_y mapping:
code: aq <- airquality[complete.cases(airquality), ] aq$TempLow <- aq$Temp - 5 aq$TempHigh <- aq$Temp + 5  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "area",     color = "lightsteelblue",     label = "area",     data = aq,     mapping = list(x_var = "Day", low_y = "TempLow", high_y = "TempHigh")   )

title: Chart Types
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "groupedBar",     color = "steelblue",     label = "grouped",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE)

title: Chart Types
text: Histograms use value mapping instead x_var/y_var:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "histogram",     color = "steelblue",     label = "hist",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(value = "mpg")   )

title: Chart Types
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "donut",     color = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73"),     label = "donut",     data = aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean),     mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")   )

title: Chart Types
text: Gauges use value mapping:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "gauge",     color = "steelblue",     label = "gauge",     data = data.frame(value = 0.75),     mapping = list(value = "value")   )

title: Chart Types
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "hexbin",     color = "steelblue",     label = "hexbin",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg", radius = 20)   )

title: Chart Types
text: Treemaps use different mapping level_1 level_2 instead x_var y_var:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "treemap",     label = "cars",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(level_1 = "vs", level_2 = "cyl")   )

title: Chart Types
text: Heatmaps display matrix values continuous color scale. axes categorical. Mapping requires x_var, y_var, value:
code: df <- expand.grid(   quarter = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"),   segment = c("Low", "Mid", "High"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df$value <- c(2, 4, 6, 5, 7, 9, 4, 6, 8, 3, 5, 7)  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "heatmap",     color = "#4E79A7",     label = "Revenue",     data = df,     mapping = list(x_var = "quarter", y_var = "segment", value = "value")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE, yAxis = TRUE) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Quarter", yLabel = "Segment")

title: Chart Types
text: Candlestick charts show open-high-low-close data. Green bars indicate close >= open, red bars indicate close < open. Mapping requires x_var, open, high, low, close:
code: df <- data.frame(   day = 1:10,   open  = c(10, 12, 11, 14, 13, 15, 14, 16, 15, 17),   high  = c(14, 15, 14, 17, 16, 18, 17, 19, 18, 20),   low   = c(9,  11, 10, 13, 12, 14, 13, 15, 14, 16),   close = c(12, 11, 14, 13, 15, 14, 16, 15, 17, 19) )  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "candlestick",     label = "Price",     data = df,     mapping = list(       x_var = "day", open = "open",       high = "high", low = "low", close = "close"     )   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xAxis = ".0f", yAxis = "$,.0f",                 xLabel = "Day", yLabel = "Price")

title: Chart Types
text: Waterfall charts show initial value affected sequential positive negative changes. cumulative transform auto-applied. Mark summary rows total mapping:
code: df <- data.frame(   step  = c("Revenue", "COGS", "Gross Profit", "OpEx", "Net Income"),   value = c(500, -200, NA, -150, NA),   is_total = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE) )  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "waterfall",     label = "P&L",     data = df,     mapping = list(x_var = "step", y_var = "value", total = "is_total")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE) |>   setAxisFormat(yAxis = "$,.0f", xLabel = "Step", yLabel = "Amount")

title: Chart Types
text: Sankey diagrams show flows nodes. Mapping requires source, target, value. standalone type mixed charts:
code: df <- data.frame(   source = c("Budget", "Budget", "Sales", "Sales", "Marketing"),   target = c("Sales", "Marketing", "Revenue", "Leads", "Leads"),   value  = c(40, 20, 30, 10, 15) )  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "sankey",     color = c("#4E79A7", "#F28E2B", "#E15759", "#76B7B2", "#59A14F"),     label = "Flow",     data = df,     mapping = list(source = "source", target = "target", value = "value")   )

title: Chart Types
text: Boxplots composite charts automatically expand area (IQR box), point (whiskers, median), optional outlier layers. Mapping requires x_var (categorical) y_var (numeric): Options: showOutliers (default TRUE), whiskerType (“tukey” “minmax”).
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "boxplot",     color = "#4E79A7",     label = "Sepal Length",     data = iris,     mapping = list(x_var = "Species", y_var = "Sepal.Length"),     options = list(showOutliers = TRUE)   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Species", yLabel = "Sepal Length (cm)")

title: Chart Types
text: Violin plots show distribution shape via mirrored kernel density estimate. expand area (density), optional box (IQR), optional median point layers: Options: showBox (TRUE), showMedian (TRUE), showPoints (FALSE), bandwidth (KDE bandwidth, default “nrd0”).
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "violin",     color = "#59A14F",     label = "Distribution",     data = iris,     mapping = list(x_var = "Species", y_var = "Sepal.Length"),     options = list(showBox = TRUE, showMedian = TRUE)   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Species", yLabel = "Sepal Length (cm)")

title: Chart Types
text: Ridgeline plots stack multiple density curves vertically comparing distributions across groups. expand one area sub-layer per group value. Mapping requires x_var (numeric values), y_var (numeric), group (categorical): Options: overlap (0-1, default 0.6), bandwidth (KDE bandwidth).
code: df <- mtcars df$cyl <- as.character(df$cyl)  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "ridgeline",     color = c("#4E79A7", "#F28E2B", "#E15759"),     label = "MPG by Cylinders",     data = df,     mapping = list(x_var = "hp", y_var = "mpg", group = "cyl"),     options = list(overlap = 0.5)   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Horsepower", yLabel = "Density")

title: Chart Types
text: can overlay compatible chart types single widget. layer points, connecting line, linear model trend line:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "steelblue",     label = "points",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     color = "orange",     label = "line",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     transform = "lm",     color = "red",     label = "trend",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   )

title: Gallery
text: gallery showcases can build myIO. example self-contained uses built-R datasets.

title: Gallery
text: Overlay raw data linear model fit:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "#E69F00",     label = "observations",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     transform = "lm",     color = "#D55E00",     label = "trend",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Weight (1000 lbs)", yLabel = "Miles per Gallon") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Track temperature across months using grouped data:
code: aq <- airquality aq$Month <- paste0("M", aq$Month)  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     color = c("#0072B2", "#E69F00", "#009E73", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7"),     label = "Month",     data = aq,     mapping = list(x_var = "Day", y_var = "Temp", group = "Month")   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Day of Month", yLabel = "Temperature (F)") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Aggregate data across categories custom formatting:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "bar",     color = "#56B4E9",     label = "avg_mpg",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE) |>   setAxisFormat(     xAxis = ".0f", yAxis = ".1f",     xLabel = "Cylinders", yLabel = "Miles per Gallon"   ) |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Visualize range using low high y values:
code: aq <- airquality[airquality$Month == 7 & complete.cases(airquality), ] aq$TempLow <- aq$Temp - 8 aq$TempHigh <- aq$Temp + 8  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "area",     color = "#56B4E9",     label = "range",     data = aq,     mapping = list(x_var = "Day", low_y = "TempLow", high_y = "TempHigh")   ) |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     color = "#0072B2",     label = "actual",     data = aq,     mapping = list(x_var = "Day", y_var = "Temp")   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Day of Month (July)", yLabel = "Temperature (F)") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Combine theming custom colors dark aesthetic:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "#48dbfb",     label = "scatter",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "hp", y_var = "qsec")   ) |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     transform = "lm",     color = "#ff6b6b",     label = "trend",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "hp", y_var = "qsec")   ) |>   setTheme(     text_color = "#b0b0b0",     grid_color = "#2d2d2d",     bg = "#0d1117",     font = "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif"   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Horsepower", yLabel = "Quarter Mile Time (s)") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Summarize proportions donut:
code: donut_data <- aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean)  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "donut",     color = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73"),     label = "cylinders",     data = donut_data,     mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")   )

title: Gallery
text: Visualize point density large datasets:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "hexbin",     color = "#0072B2",     label = "density",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg", radius = 20)   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Weight (1000 lbs)", yLabel = "Miles per Gallon") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Show distribution mean reference line:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "histogram",     color = "#CC79A7",     label = "distribution",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(value = "mpg")   ) |>   setReferenceLines(xRef = mean(mtcars$mpg))

title: Gallery
text: Enable drag interactions exploratory analysis:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "#009E73",     label = "draggable",     data = mtcars[1:10, ],     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   dragPoints() |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Weight", yLabel = "MPG") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Explore hierarchical data:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "treemap",     label = "cars",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(level_1 = "vs", level_2 = "cyl")   )

title: Gallery
text: Visualize matrix values continuous color:
code: df <- expand.grid(   quarter = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"),   segment = c("Enterprise", "Mid-Market", "SMB"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df$revenue <- c(120, 145, 160, 155, 80, 95, 110, 105, 40, 55, 65, 60)  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "heatmap",     color = "#4E79A7",     label = "Revenue",     data = df,     mapping = list(x_var = "quarter", y_var = "segment", value = "revenue")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE, yAxis = TRUE) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Quarter", yLabel = "Segment")

title: Gallery
text: Show open-high-low-close financial data:
code: df <- data.frame(   day   = 1:8,   open  = c(44, 45, 43, 46, 45, 47, 46, 48),   high  = c(47, 48, 46, 49, 48, 50, 49, 52),   low   = c(43, 44, 42, 45, 44, 46, 45, 47),   close = c(45, 43, 46, 45, 47, 46, 48, 51) )  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "candlestick",     label = "Stock",     data = df,     mapping = list(       x_var = "day", open = "open",       high = "high", low = "low", close = "close"     )   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xAxis = ".0f", yAxis = "$,.0f",                 xLabel = "Trading Day", yLabel = "Price") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Show values accumulate sequential steps:
code: df <- data.frame(   step     = c("Revenue", "COGS", "Gross Profit", "OpEx", "Tax", "Net Income"),   value    = c(500, -200, NA, -120, -30, NA),   is_total = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE) )  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "waterfall",     label = "P&L Bridge",     data = df,     mapping = list(x_var = "step", y_var = "value", total = "is_total")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE) |>   setAxisFormat(yAxis = "$,.0f", xLabel = "Step", yLabel = "Amount") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

text: Visualize flows nodes:

text: Compare distributions across groups:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "boxplot",     color = "#4E79A7",     label = "Petal Width",     data = iris,     mapping = list(x_var = "Species", y_var = "Petal.Width")   ) |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Species", yLabel = "Petal Width (cm)") |>   setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)

title: Gallery
text: Show distribution shape alongside summary statistics:

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: myIO R package combines R D3.js create interactive data visualizations using htmlwidgets framework. Charts built myIO work RStudio, Shiny apps, R Markdown documents. core workflow simple: Create widget myIO() Add layers addIoLayer() Customize option functions

title: Getting Started with myIO
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("mortonanalytics/myIO")

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: Create scatter plot mtcars data:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "#E69F00",     label = "mpg_vs_wt",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   )

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: can stack layers single chart. add points linear model trend line using lm transform: See Transforms & Theming vignette details available transforms.
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "steelblue",     label = "points",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     transform = "lm",     color = "red",     label = "trend",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   )

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: Option functions let control axes, margins, legends, theming, . follow pipe-friendly pattern:
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "bar",     color = "orange",     label = "bars",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE) |>   setAxisFormat(xAxis = ".0f", yAxis = ".0f") |>   setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Cylinders", yLabel = "Miles per Gallon") |>   setMargin(top = 40, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10) |>   setTransitionSpeed(speed = 500)

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: data grouping variable, pass mapping automatically create one layer per group:
code: aq <- airquality aq$Month <- paste0("M", aq$Month)  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     color = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#D55E00", "#0072B2"),     label = "Month",     data = aq,     mapping = list(x_var = "Day", y_var = "Temp", group = "Month")   )

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: Apply visual themes change colors, fonts, backgrounds: See Transforms & Theming full theming API.
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "#56B4E9",     label = "scatter",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   setTheme(     text_color = "#e0e0e0",     grid_color = "#333333",     bg = "#1a1a2e",     font = "Fira Code, monospace"   )

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: myIO provides standard Shiny bindings: - myIOOutput() UI - renderMyIO() server
code: library(shiny)  ui <- fluidPage(   myIOOutput("my_chart", width = "100%", height = "400px") )  server <- function(input, output) {   output$my_chart <- renderMyIO({     myIO() |>       addIoLayer(         type = "point",         color = "steelblue",         label = "scatter",         data = mtcars,         mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")       )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with myIO
text: myIO supports 17 chart types. See Chart Types vignette examples . Composite types automatically expand multiple sub-layers using appropriate transforms (density, quantiles, etc.).

title: Shiny Integration
text: myIO provides standard htmlwidgets Shiny bindings, charts work like output Shiny app. vignette covers basics common patterns.

title: Shiny Integration
text: Use myIOOutput() UI renderMyIO() server:
code: library(shiny) library(myIO)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("myIO + Shiny"),   myIOOutput("chart", width = "100%", height = "400px") )  server <- function(input, output) {   output$chart <- renderMyIO({     myIO() |>       addIoLayer(         type = "point",         color = "steelblue",         label = "scatter",         data = mtcars,         mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")       )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Shiny Integration
text: renderMyIO() reactive, chart updates automatically inputs change. Use standard Shiny reactivity filter transform data:
code: library(shiny) library(myIO)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Reactive myIO Chart"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput("cyl", "Cylinders:",         choices = c("All", sort(unique(mtcars$cyl))),         selected = "All"       ),       selectInput("chart_type", "Chart Type:",         choices = c("point", "bar", "line"),         selected = "point"       )     ),     mainPanel(       myIOOutput("chart", height = "500px")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output) {   filtered_data <- reactive({     if (input$cyl == "All") mtcars else mtcars[mtcars$cyl == as.numeric(input$cyl), ]   })    output$chart <- renderMyIO({     myIO() |>       addIoLayer(         type = input$chart_type,         color = "#E69F00",         label = "data",         data = filtered_data(),         mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")       ) |>       setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Weight (1000 lbs)", yLabel = "Miles per Gallon") |>       setMargin(top = 20, bottom = 70, left = 60, right = 10)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Shiny Integration
text: Add multiple myIOOutput() calls display several charts side side. chart gets output ID:
code: library(shiny) library(myIO)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Multiple Charts"),   fluidRow(     column(6, myIOOutput("scatter", height = "350px")),     column(6, myIOOutput("bars", height = "350px"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output) {   output$scatter <- renderMyIO({     myIO() |>       addIoLayer(         type = "point",         color = "#56B4E9",         label = "scatter",         data = mtcars,         mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")       ) |>       setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Weight", yLabel = "MPG")   })    output$bars <- renderMyIO({     myIO() |>       addIoLayer(         type = "bar",         color = "#E69F00",         label = "bars",         data = mtcars,         mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")       ) |>       defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE) |>       setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Cylinders", yLabel = "MPG")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Shiny Integration
text: Theme tokens setTheme() work way Shiny. makes easy match chart app’s look feel:
code: library(shiny) library(myIO)  ui <- fluidPage(   style = "background-color: #1a1a2e; color: #e0e0e0;",   titlePanel("Dark Mode Dashboard"),   myIOOutput("chart", height = "400px") )  server <- function(input, output) {   output$chart <- renderMyIO({     myIO() |>       addIoLayer(         type = "line",         color = c("#48dbfb", "#ff6b6b", "#feca57", "#ff9ff3", "#00d2ff"),         label = "Month",         data = within(airquality, Month <- paste0("M", Month)),         mapping = list(x_var = "Day", y_var = "Temp", group = "Month")       ) |>       setTheme(         text_color = "#b0b0b0",         grid_color = "#2d2d2d",         bg = "#1a1a2e",         font = "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif"       ) |>       setAxisFormat(xLabel = "Day", yLabel = "Temperature")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Shiny Integration
text: myIOOutput() accepts width height CSS units. Charts responsive default adapt container:
code: # Fixed size myIOOutput("chart1", width = "600px", height = "400px")  # Fill container width myIOOutput("chart2", width = "100%", height = "500px")

title: Shiny Integration
text: full myIO chart gallery deployed Shiny app. Try 17 chart types interactively: Open new tab

title: Shiny Integration
text: Transitions: Use setTransitionSpeed(speed = 0) disable animations chart updates frequently reactive inputs. Export: Charts include built-CSV SVG export buttons work Shiny without extra configuration. Drag points: dragPoints() enables interactive point dragging. Shiny context, can use exploratory -analysis.

title: Transforms & Theming
text: Transforms derive new data existing layer rendering. specify transform transform argument addIoLayer().

title: Transforms & Theming
text: Transforms marked “internal” applied automatically composite chart types. typically use "identity" (default) "lm" directly.

title: Transforms & Theming
text: Add trend line scatter plot combining "point" layer "line" layer uses transform = "lm": lm transform fits y ~ x using stats::lm() replaces y values fitted values. resulting line sorted x renders smooth trend.
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "#E69F00",     label = "points",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     transform = "lm",     color = "red",     label = "trend",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   )

title: Transforms & Theming
text: "cumulative" transform auto-applied use type = "waterfall". computes running totals produces _base_y _cumulative_y columns waterfall renderer uses floating bars:
code: df <- data.frame(   step  = c("Start", "Sales", "Returns", "Total"),   value = c(100, 50, -20, NA),   is_total = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE) )  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "waterfall",     label = "bridge",     data = df,     mapping = list(x_var = "step", y_var = "value", total = "is_total")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE)

title: Transforms & Theming
text: every transform works every chart type. table shows valid combinations: pass incompatible combination, addIoLayer() error clear message.

title: Transforms & Theming
text: Use setTheme() customize chart appearance CSS custom properties.

title: Transforms & Theming
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "point",     color = "#56B4E9",     label = "scatter",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "wt", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   setTheme(     text_color = "#e0e0e0",     grid_color = "#333333",     bg = "#1a1a2e",     font = "Fira Code, monospace"   )

title: Transforms & Theming
text: Pass additional CSS custom properties via ... (chart- prefix added automatically):
code: myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "bar",     color = "coral",     label = "bars",     data = mtcars,     mapping = list(x_var = "cyl", y_var = "mpg")   ) |>   defineCategoricalAxis(xAxis = TRUE) |>   setTheme(     bg = "#fafafa",     text_color = "#333",     "border-radius" = "8px"   )

title: Transforms & Theming
text: Combine theming color choices dark-mode chart:
code: aq <- airquality aq$Month <- paste0("M", aq$Month)  myIO() |>   addIoLayer(     type = "line",     color = c("#00d2ff", "#ff6b6b", "#feca57", "#48dbfb", "#ff9ff3"),     label = "Month",     data = aq,     mapping = list(x_var = "Day", y_var = "Temp", group = "Month")   ) |>   setTheme(     text_color = "#b0b0b0",     grid_color = "#2d2d2d",     bg = "#0d1117",     font = "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif"   )

title: myIO 1.0.0
text: Layered grammar composing visualizations transform-aware addIoLayer(), including transform = "lm" derived line layers. Chart types: bar, grouped bar, line, area, scatter, histogram, hexbin, treemap, gauge, donut charts. Versioned widget spec per-layer ids, transform metadata, source-key provenance. Interactive tooltips automatic formatting via setToolTipOptions(). Responsive sizing adapts container dimensions. CSV SVG export buttons. Reference lines support via setReferenceLines(). Drag interaction support via dragPoints(). Shiny integration myIOOutput() renderMyIO(). Pipe-friendly API using native |>. Option setters: setAxisFormat(), setAxisLimits(), setMargin(), setColorScheme(), setTheme(), setTransitionSpeed(), defineCategoricalAxis(), setToggle(), flipAxis(), suppressAxis(), suppressLegend().
